_comment: Shared facts rendered across all pages. Every fact has a source. Flagged fields must be confirmed before publish. last_verified is rendered on-page.

form.name: Uniform Mitigation Verification Inspection Form
form.basis: 2024 Residential Wind-Loss Mitigation Study commissioned by the Florida Office of Insurance Regulation
form.insurers_apply_new_credits: 2026-07 (approximate; carriers expected to begin applying credits based on the new form around July 2026)
form.validity_note: Valid up to five years provided no material changes are made to the structure and no inaccuracies are found
key_changes: Stricter documentation: photos, product approval numbers, and permit records required for credited features
Features that cannot be documented through a permit or clear visual inspection generally will not receive credit
Discount tables updated to current building science; some features may earn more, others less, under stricter criteria
Reports completed before April 2026 may remain valid up to five years, but improvements made since the last inspection may warrant a new inspection under the updated form

statutes.discount_mandate: Florida Statute 627.0629 requires residential property insurers to discount the windstorm portion of premiums based on documented mitigation features; OIR must review and update fixtures, techniques, and related discounts by Jan 1, 2025 and every 5 years after
statutes.insurer_disclosure: Effective Oct 1, 2023, all residential property insurers must describe available hurricane mitigation discounts on their websites (s. 627.0629)
statutes.offer_requirement: Florida Statute 627.711 requires insurers to offer discounts when hurricane-resistant construction features are documented

inspection.cost_note: Typical local cost; prices may rise under the more documentation-heavy 2026 form
inspection.duration: under an hour for most homes (longer under the 2026 form)
inspection.discount_note: Discounts apply only to the windstorm portion of the premium. Ranges vary by home, county, and carrier. Never present as guaranteed.

credit_features.detail: Hip roof earns the strongest shape credit
Roof or home built under the 2001 Florida Building Code (effective March 1, 2002) or later earns baseline credit
Impact glass or exterior shutters. All-or-nothing rule: every opening must be protected to earn the credit. Interior treatments do not qualify.

msfh.name: My Safe Florida Home
msfh.administrator: Florida Department of Financial Services (FL DFS)
msfh.status: Open, subject to available funding. Reopened 2025-08-04 for the 2025-26 cycle. Demand exceeds funding; applications queue by priority group, first-come first-served within group.
msfh.current_cycle_funding_usd: NEEDS_VERIFICATION: sources conflict between $280M and $352M for the 2025-26 cycle. Verify against mysafeflhome.com or the General Appropriations Act before publishing a number.
msfh.backlog: Approximately 45,000 homeowners with completed inspections were awaiting grants as of mid-2026 (NEEDS_VERIFICATION against current DFS reporting before publish)
msfh.fy2026_27: Florida lawmakers reached a budget deal (floor vote 2026-05-29) reappropriating over $405M in unused funds across MSFH and the My Safe Florida Condo Pilot, aimed largely at the inspection-to-grant backlog. Signing expected June 2026. NEEDS_VERIFICATION: confirm signature and final amounts.
msfh.tax_package: Separate ~$150M tax-relief package includes a three-year sales tax exemption on impact windows and doors. NEEDS_VERIFICATION: confirm enactment and effective dates.
structure: Free program-assigned wind mitigation inspection for eligible homeowners
Matching grant for improvements recommended in that inspection report. Reimbursement after completed, inspected work. Not a loan.

grant_terms.match: 2:1 state match (state pays $2 per $1 homeowner spends) up to the cap
grant_terms.low_income_exception: Low-income homeowners may receive up to $10,000 without the matching requirement

eligibility.property: Site-built, single-family, owner-occupied primary residence with homestead exemption
eligibility.build_year: Built before 2008 (program rule references permit/build predating Jan 1, 2008)
eligibility.insured_value_note: Insurance Coverage A at or below $700,000; cap waived for low-income applicants per program materials (NEEDS_VERIFICATION on the waiver detail)
eligibility.insurance_required: Must carry a valid homeowners insurance policy
eligibility.inspection_first: A program wind mitigation inspection must be completed before a grant is issued; only improvements recommended in the inspection report are eligible
eligibility.income_limit_this_cycle: 2025-26 cycle limited to low- and moderate-income homeowners. Higher-income homeowners are not eligible this cycle (a change from earlier years).

income_tiers.low: Household income at or below 80% of county median income
income_tiers.moderate: Above 80% and at or below 120% of county median income

eligibility.application_window_rule: For the first 60 days of a new application period, priority groups apply; after that window the general (eligible) pool opens. Once funding runs out, applicants are waitlisted for the next cycle.

eligible_improvements: Opening protection: impact-rated windows, impact-rated exterior doors, hurricane shutters, qualifying garage doors (only openings flagged in the inspection report)
msfh.contractor_rule: No set MSFH contractor list since mid-2024; homeowners may use any Florida state-certified contractor. Verify licenses at myfloridalicense.com (DBPR). Do not sign a contract or begin work before grant approval.

outcomes.note: Voluntary reporting; savings are not guaranteed and typically require comprehensive hardening rather than a single feature. Always present with this caveat.

changelog.entry: FY2026-27 budget reappropriating $405M+ across MSFH and the Condo Pilot awaits the Governor's signature (expected June 2026).
Application portal reopened for the 2025-26 cycle, limited to low- and moderate-income homeowners, with age/income priority groups.
New priority-group ordering took effect (low-income 60+ first).

monetization.profitise_embed: PLACEHOLDER: Jack supplies the publisher embed snippet
monetization.consent_language_status: DRAFT ONLY: TCPA-style consent copy requires legal review before any lead form goes live
